---
name: auth-typeorm-conventions
description: >
  TypeORM entity conventions for the auth project.
  Trigger: When creating a new TypeORM entity, adding columns or relations,
  creating migrations, or working with the DataSource configuration.
license: Apache-2.0
metadata:
  author: gentleman-programming
  version: "1.0"
---

## When to Use

- Creating a new `XxxEntity` class
- Adding columns or relations to an existing entity
- Writing or running migrations
- Modifying `migrations.data-source.ts` or `seed.data-source.ts`

## Critical Patterns

### PrimaryColumn UUID — ID is generated by the domain, not the DB

```typescript
// CORRECT — domain generates the UUID
@PrimaryColumn('uuid')
id: string;

// WRONG — never use generated columns
@PrimaryGeneratedColumn('uuid')
id: string;
```

The `id` is always generated by `uuidv4()` in the domain's `create()` method. The DB receives it already generated.

### synchronize: false — always

```typescript
// migrations.data-source.ts
synchronize: false,
```

TypeORM never auto-modifies the schema. All changes go through migrations (`src/db/migrations/`). Never set `synchronize: true` even in development.

### JoinTable with synchronize: false

Junction tables (`user_role`, `role_permission`) are managed exclusively via migrations:

```typescript
@JoinTable({
  name: 'role_permission',
  joinColumn: { name: 'role_id' },
  inverseJoinColumn: { name: 'permission_id' },
  synchronize: false,  // CRITICAL — never remove this
})
```

### Entity structure: toDomain() + fromDomain()

Every entity has exactly these two conversion methods:

```typescript
@Entity('permission')
export class PermissionEntity {
  @PrimaryColumn('uuid')
  id: string;

  @Column()
  code: string;

  @CreateDateColumn({ name: 'created_at' })
  createdAt: Date;

  // Instance method: entity → domain object
  toDomain(): Permission {
    return Permission.create({
      id: this.id,
      code: this.code,
      createdAt: this.createdAt,
    });
  }

  // Static factory: domain object → entity
  static fromDomain(permission: Permission): PermissionEntity {
    const primitive = permission.toPrimitive();
    const entity = new PermissionEntity();
    entity.id = primitive.id;
    entity.code = primitive.code;
    return entity;
  }
}
```

`fromDomain()` uses `toPrimitive()` to access domain data — never accesses private attributes directly.

### Audit columns

| Field | Decorator | DB column name |
|-------|-----------|----------------|
| `createdAt` | `@CreateDateColumn` | `created_at` |
| `updatedAt` | `@UpdateDateColumn` | `updated_at` |
| `deletedAt` | `@DeleteDateColumn` | `deleted_at` |

`deletedAt` is only used in `UserAccountEntity` for soft-delete. Do not add it to other entities unless explicitly required.

### eager: true on relations

```typescript
@ManyToMany(() => PermissionEntity, { eager: true })
@JoinTable({ ... })
permissions: PermissionEntity[];
```

All relations use `eager: true`. No need for explicit `relations: [...]` in queries.

### Column naming: snake_case in DB

All multi-word column names use snake_case in the database:

```typescript
@Column({ name: 'user_account_id' })    // FK columns
@CreateDateColumn({ name: 'created_at' })
```

### Entity auto-discovery

Entities are discovered automatically via glob in `migrations.data-source.ts`:
```typescript
entities: [__dirname + '/../**/infrastructure/entities/*{.ts,.js}']
```

Any `@Entity()` class inside `infrastructure/entities/` is registered automatically. No manual registration needed in modules.

### Two DataSources

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/db/migrations.data-source.ts` | Schema migrations |
| `src/db/seed.data-source.ts` | Seed data (reuses `DataSourceOptions`) |

## Commands

```bash
# Create a new migration
pnpm migration:create src/db/migrations/MigrationName

# Run migrations
pnpm migration:run

# Run seeds
pnpm seed:run

# Revert last seed
pnpm seed:revert
```
